brand.name: Peak Form
brand.tagline: Your body. Your gym. Your rules.

new_product.name: Smart Jump Rope with Companion App

act1.title: Audience Discovery
act1.question: Our customers love us because we're cheap and simple. Will they pay $129 for a rope?
act1.context: Peak Form connects Shopify + Meta. LCBM analyzes 60K band customers for smart fitness device propensity.

bridge_segment.name: Tech-Curious Athletes
bridge_segment.description: Band customers who also own an Apple Watch or Whoop
bridge_segment.insight: They like simple equipment but track everything digitally — the bridge between worlds

act1.social_agents_insight: Your band customers chose you BECAUSE you're not a tech company. Launching a $129 smart device might confuse them. But the jump rope community and HIIT crowd have been waiting for exactly this product — they just don't know Peak Form.

new_audiences.name: CrossFit Home Gymers
new_audiences.psychographics: Competitive even in solo training, invested in home gym, performance-obsessed

affinities.name: CrossFit Content
affinities.reason: Jump rope is a core CrossFit skill (double-unders)

affinities.name: Home Gym Setup Content
affinities.reason: Already invested in premium home equipment

affinities.name: Performance Tracking
affinities.reason: Tracks every lift, every PR, every rep

new_audiences.name: HIIT Enthusiasts
new_audiences.psychographics: Cardio-focused, class-going mentality at home, loves interval training

affinities.name: HIIT Workout Videos
affinities.reason: Jump rope is a perfect HIIT tool

new_audiences.recommended_angle: Maximum calorie burn in minimum time

new_audiences.name: Fitness App Power Users
new_audiences.psychographics: Data-obsessed, quantified self movement, tracks sleep, steps, HRV
new_audiences.recommended_angle: The missing data layer for your fitness stack

new_audiences.name: Jump Rope Community
new_audiences.psychographics: Jump rope is an identity, not just exercise. Passionate, community-driven, skill-obsessed
new_audiences.recommended_angle: The smartest rope the community has ever seen

strategies.label: Performance & Data First
strategies.description: Lead with the data story. Target fitness app users, CrossFit crowd, and HIIT enthusiasts who already track everything and want to add jump rope data to their stack.
strategies.target: CrossFit Home Gymers, HIIT Enthusiasts, Fitness App Power Users
strategies.pitch: Beat Your Yesterday — track every rep, every RPM, every streak
strategies.risks: HIIT and app users may churn if they don't build the jump rope habit first

strategies.label: Jump Rope Community First
strategies.description: Go deep with the jump rope community first. 800K passionate, engaged people who've been waiting for a smart rope. Let them become ambassadors before scaling.
strategies.target: Jump Rope Community + CrossFit Home Gymers
strategies.pitch: Built for the jump rope community. Finally.
strategies.risks: Smaller initial reach, community can be critical if product doesn't deliver

act2.title: Creative Bake-off
act2.question: Our best UGC creator does living-room band workouts. That won't sell a smart rope.
act2.context: 12 creatives submitted. Some use Peak Form's accessible style, others go hard on tech and data.

creatives.name: Beat Your Yesterday
creatives.description: Split-screen Day 1 vs Day 30. RPM data climbing, streak counter growing, visible transformation
creatives.hook: Day 1: 45 RPM. Day 30: 142 RPM. Same rope. Different you.
creatives.why_it_works: Taps into quantified-self motivation. Data progression is viscerally satisfying.
creatives.why_it_fails: Existing band customers don't identify with data-heavy fitness culture.

creatives.name: First Session Unboxing
creatives.description: Raw unboxing, first cable setup, first 60 seconds of jumping, app pairing
creatives.hook: I finally got the Peak Form smart rope. First impressions...
creatives.why_it_works: Jump rope community wants to see the product in action, not a lifestyle pitch. Authenticity wins.
creatives.why_it_fails: Too product-focused for lifestyle audiences who need inspiration first.

creatives.name: One More Thing
creatives.description: Familiar Peak Form creator adds smart rope to her band routine, shows app stats after
creatives.hook: I added one thing to my Peak Form routine and my cardio completely changed
creatives.why_it_works: Familiar face bridges the gap between bands and rope for existing fans.
creatives.why_it_fails: New audiences don't know the creator. No compelling hook for cold traffic.

creatives.name: Double Under in 7 Days
creatives.description: Creator goes from zero to double-unders in 7 days using the app's guided program
creatives.hook: I learned double-unders in 7 days. Here's exactly how.
creatives.why_it_works: Skill progression is the #1 motivator for the jump rope community. App as coach angle.
creatives.why_it_fails: Too niche for general fitness audiences who don't know what double-unders are.

creatives.name: Calorie Crusher
creatives.description: Data comparison — 10 minutes jump rope vs 30 minutes treadmill. RPM counter. Calorie burn.
creatives.hook: 10 minutes. 147 calories. The most efficient cardio you're not doing.
creatives.why_it_works: HIIT crowd loves efficiency data. Positions rope as the smart cardio choice.
creatives.why_it_fails: Band customers chose simplicity over optimization — data overwhelms them.

creatives.name: Workout Anywhere
creatives.description: Jump rope in hotel room, park, garage gym — Peak Form's accessible ethos applied to smart rope
creatives.hook: Your gym is wherever you are. Now it's smarter.
creatives.why_it_works: Connects Peak Form's 'workout anywhere' DNA to the new product. Brand consistency.
creatives.why_it_fails: Lifestyle framing too soft for CrossFit and data-obsessed audiences.

performance_first.landing_page: Data-forward page — app dashboard preview, RPM tracking, streak system

community_first.landing_page: Community-first page — creator seeding, skill progression, app as coach
community_first.tiktok_note: Jump rope community TikTok seeding with 5 micro-creators. First TikTok activation for Peak Form.

act3.title: Performance Diagnosis
act3.question: The jump rope community loves us. Everyone else bounced after the free trial.
act3.context: Six weeks in. Jump rope community is a home run. Everyone else is churning.

tracks.name: Jump Rope Community
tracks.insight: Home run — they already have the habit, the app supercharges it

tracks.name: HIIT & CrossFit
tracks.insight: Buying the rope but abandoning the app within 2 weeks

tracks.name: Existing Band Customers
tracks.insight: Wrong audience for this product — they chose Peak Form for simplicity

tracks.name: Fitness App Users
tracks.insight: Downloading app but not building jump rope habit

diagnosis.title: Habit Formation Gap
diagnosis.not_the_problem: Acquisition metrics are fine — the creative is converting
diagnosis.root_cause: LCBM analyzes app engagement data: non-jump-rope people don't have a jumping habit. The app assumes you already know what a double-under is. First session experience is intimidating for beginners.
diagnosis.funnel_insight: App drop-off analysis: 71% of non-community users quit after Session 1. Exit survey: 'I didn't know where to start' and 'It felt too advanced for me'
diagnosis.social_agents: You're selling a habit-forming product to people who don't have the habit yet. The jump rope community doesn't need onboarding — they've been jumping for years. Everyone else needs a 'Week 1 for Beginners' guided program before they see the app's real value.

recovery_strategies.label: Day 1 to Day 30 Challenge
recovery_strategies.description: Create a structured beginner program in the app. New ad creative that sets expectations: 'Zero experience needed. The app teaches you.' Retarget churned users with the Day 1 Challenge.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Zero experience needed. The app teaches you everything — starting with your first 60 seconds.
recovery_strategies.app_change: New 'Absolute Beginner' track added to app onboarding. Day 1 is just 3 minutes of basic jumping.

recovery_strategies.label: Pivot Budget to Community
recovery_strategies.description: Stop HIIT and App User campaigns entirely. Double down on jump rope community and CrossFit where retention is proven. Reallocate 70% of budget.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Built for the jump rope community. Backed by data. Ready for your next PR.

tracks.name: Jump Rope Community
tracks.insight: Exactly as predicted — community loves it and stays

tracks.name: CrossFit Home Gymers
tracks.insight: CrossFit crowd has some jump rope familiarity — retention is solid

tracks.name: Existing Band Customers
tracks.insight: Same issue — band customers aren't the smart rope audience

diagnosis.title: Scale Ceiling Approaching
diagnosis.not_the_problem: Community strategy is working brilliantly — retention is best in class
diagnosis.root_cause: The jump rope community is 800K strong — you've reached 12% of them already. CrossFit is scaling well but the unit economics aren't as strong. Need to expand beyond the core community without losing what makes the product great.
diagnosis.funnel_insight: Community ambassador program is organically growing — 340 creators have posted about Peak Form smart rope without paid promotion. This is the expansion engine.
diagnosis.social_agents: You've won the community. Now let the community win everyone else. Your ambassadors are your best acquisition channel — give them tools, not just product.

recovery_strategies.label: Ambassador-Led Expansion
recovery_strategies.description: Formalize the 340 organic creators into a paid ambassador program. They create content targeting HIIT and fitness app audiences — with community credibility that ads can't buy.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: 10,000 jump rope athletes already switched. Here's why the HIIT community is next.
recovery_strategies.expected_lift: Reach expansion 3x, HIIT retention improves to 48% with community-endorsed content

recovery_strategies.label: Build the Beginner Bridge
recovery_strategies.description: Launch a beginner program specifically designed to bring HIIT and general fitness audiences into the jump rope habit. Community creators teach beginners — bridging the gap.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: The jump rope community's best kept secret — now with a beginner's guide.

act4.title: Regional Expansion
act4.question: Online is working. Now we're going into Dick's Sporting Goods in 3 test markets.
act4.context: Peak Form is entering retail in Dallas, Chicago, and Miami. Each market has a different fitness culture.

markets.name: Dallas
markets.fitness_culture: CrossFit density is 2x the national average
markets.competitive_landscape: Rogue and Concept2 dominate premium fitness. Smart devices growing.
markets.lcbm_insight: CrossFit density is 2x the national average in Dallas. The HIIT and CrossFit audience that underperformed nationally is highly concentrated here. Lead with competitive motivation and performance data.
markets.dicks_strategy: End-cap display near CrossFit and functional fitness equipment. QR code to PR tracking challenge.

playbook.awareness: CrossFit and HIIT competitive content — PR tracking, RPM leaderboards
playbook.consideration: In-store demo station with live RPM tracking display
playbook.conversion: 30-day challenge QR code with Dick's exclusive bundle

beginner_friendly.advantage: Beginner program removes the intimidation factor for non-CrossFit Dallas buyers
beginner_friendly.extra_tactic: In-store 'Start Your Journey' display with beginner challenge QR code

community_authority.advantage: Dallas CrossFit community responds to peer validation — leverage ambassador content
community_authority.extra_tactic: Local CrossFit box partnership — Peak Form as official jump rope partner

markets.name: Chicago
markets.fitness_culture: Long winters drive indoor fitness demand heavily
markets.competitive_landscape: Indoor fitness brands dominate. Home gym market is large.
markets.lcbm_insight: Long winters make indoor fitness a necessity, not a choice. The 'workout anywhere' angle that didn't work nationally resonates strongly here. When it's -10 outside, Peak Form's home workout DNA is a superpower.
markets.dicks_strategy: Winter fitness section placement. 'No gym? No problem.' seasonal messaging.

playbook.awareness: Winter indoor fitness content — apartment workouts, garage gym, basement gym
playbook.consideration: Value comparison — smart rope vs gym membership cost over winter
playbook.conversion: Drive to Dick's with winter bundle — rope + cold weather workout guide

beginner_friendly.advantage: Chicago's indoor fitness crowd includes many beginners — beginner program is key
beginner_friendly.extra_tactic: 30-day indoor winter challenge campaign launching November

ambassador_powered.advantage: Chicago fitness creators are highly local and trusted — perfect for ambassador activation
ambassador_powered.extra_tactic: Chicago fitness influencer winter series — smart rope as the indoor cardio hero

markets.name: Miami
markets.competitive_landscape: Outdoor and water sports brands strong. Portability is key purchase driver.
markets.lcbm_insight: Outdoor fitness culture means the smart rope is an outdoor product here, not indoor. Completely different use case than Chicago. Imagery must shift from living rooms and garages to parks, beaches, and waterfronts.
markets.dicks_strategy: Outdoor sports section placement. 'Take it outside' Miami-specific display.

playbook.awareness: Outdoor workout content — beach, park, waterfront with smart rope
playbook.consideration: Portability and weather-resistance focus. Tracks outdoor workouts.
playbook.conversion: Miami exclusive: outdoor workout challenge with local fitness community

beginner_friendly.advantage: Miami's tourist and newcomer population means many beginner fitness seekers
beginner_friendly.extra_tactic: Beach workout series with beginner jump rope program — shareable content

community_authority.advantage: Miami's outdoor fitness community is tight-knit and influential
community_authority.extra_tactic: South Beach jump rope community meetup sponsored by Peak Form

layers.name: Retail Drive-to-Store
layers.description: Location-targeted ads driving to nearest Dick's Sporting Goods

layers.name: App Acquisition
layers.description: QR codes in-store deep-linking to beginner challenge or community hub

layers.name: DTC Retargeting
layers.description: Online browsers who visited DTC site retargeted with retail availability

layers.name: Community Building
layers.description: City-specific ambassador content building local Peak Form culture
